records.ety: [Cf. AS. stedig sterile, barren, staeððig, steady (in gestaeððig), D. stedig, stadig, steeg, G. stätig, stetig. See Stead, n.]

defs: Firm in standing or position; not tottering or shaking; fixed; firm.
quotes: Their feet steady, their hands diligent, their eyes watchful, and their hearts resolute.

defs: Constant in feeling, purpose, or pursuit; not fickle, changeable, or wavering; not easily moved or persuaded to alter a purpose; resolute; as, a man steady in his principles, in his purpose, or in the pursuit of an object.
Regular; constant; undeviating; uniform; as, the steady course of the sun; a steady breeze of wind.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Steadied (-d); p. pr. & vb. n. Steadying.]

defs: To make steady; to hold or keep from shaking, reeling, or falling; to make or keep firm; to support; to make constant, regular, or resolute.
To become steady; to regain a steady position or state; to move steadily.
quotes: Without a breeze, without a tide, She steadies with upright keel.
